role_summary: Implements USB Type-C sink-side physical interface and PD negotiation support.
Generates a protected switched sink output rail named VSINK from VBUS.
Exports I2C access to the STUSB4500 controller through an external connector.
Exports a USB2 differential pair from the receptacle/protection path to an external board.

key_devices.function: USB-C PD sink controller / policy manager
design_relevance: Own CC1/CC2 attachment and PD negotiation logic
Conditions CC and D+/D- signals before they leave the board

firmware_implications: The ESP32 is unlikely to implement USB-PD directly; the STUSB4500 performs the PD role.
Firmware may interact with this board over I2C if the board-to-board ST_SDA/ST_SCL nets reach the main MCU.
VSINK is a power-domain output, not an MCU logic signal.
The board may provide a fault indication path via the FLT node, but its system-level destination is not visible inside this 3-page schematic alone.
open_points: Cross-board mapping is still needed to prove where J2 lands in the overall system.
J2 pins 03/04/06/09 are not clearly assigned in visible sheet content and may require CAD-origin verification if they matter later.
